rationale: Target identified as the centrally-located gray-shingle hip-roofed house in the image (lat/lng 37.0290, -93.2833, Nixa MO). The roof is oriented with its long axis running roughly NW–SE at ~30° off north. Bounding box estimated as follows: Main body from approximately pixel (395,450) to (635,660) → 240 px wide × 210 px deep; a southwest-facing front wing/addition from approx (390,635) to (558,722) → 168 px wide × 87 px deep. Main body footprint (trapezoidal hip, ~85% of bounding box): 240×210×0.85 = 42,840 sq px. Wing footprint: 168×87×0.85 = 12,407 sq px. Combined minus small interior seam (~1,200 sq px): ≈54,047 sq px × 0.038225 sq ft/sq px ≈ 2,066 sq ft → rounded to 2,080 sq ft. This falls comfortably within the 1,500–4,000 sq ft sanity range. The roof appears to be a fully hipped configuration (no exposed gable rakes visible), so Rake = 0. Ridge lines are short due to near-square proportions: main ridge ≈120 px = 23 ft; wing ridge ≈80 px = 15 ft → total 38 ft. Hip lines: 4 hips on main body each ≈92 px = 18 ft (×4 = 72 ft); 2–3 hips on wing each ≈65 px = 12.7 ft (×2 = 22 ft) → total 94 ft. Valleys occur at the two re-entrant junctions where the wing meets the main body: 2 valleys × ~56 px = 22 ft. Eave equals the outer perimeter of the combined T-shaped footprint: back (N) 240 px, sides 2×210 px, wing steps 2×36 px, wing sides 2×87 px, wing front 168 px ≈ 1,074 px − minor interior connection 180 px = 894 px × 0.1955 ft/px ≈ 174 ft. Confidence is moderate-low due to tree occlusion of roof edges and uncertainty in precisely which structure corresponds to the given address.
